zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Motte -Wood於大約1564年出生在英國多瑟特萊 雷吉時，她的父親Richard Edward Atwood25歲，母親Ellen Bosworth30歲。她於大約1588年與John Wood VI在英國英格蘭賀特福爾賀特福德結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1636年在英國萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Motte -Wood于大约1564年出生在英国多瑟特萊 雷吉时，她的父亲Richard Edward Atwood25岁，母亲Ellen Bosworth30岁。她于大约1588年与John Wood VI在英国英格兰賀特福爾賀特福德结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1636年在英国萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: Motte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Edouard, Etienne, Monique, Yves.  German Klaus, Reinhard.

French, Walloon, and Flemish: topographic name from Old French motte ‘motte’, a word of Gaulish origin denoting a man-made protective mound surrounding a castle or other fortified stronghold; or a habitational name from any of the places in France and in Belgium named with this word. Compare Delamotte , Demotte , and Lamotte .
English: variant of   Mott   1.

variants.name: Delamotte
meanings: French and Walloon: variant of Lamotte , with fused preposition de ‘from’.

History: Joseph de la Motte, born at Tournai, Belgium, fled to Geneva following the persecution of Protestants by the Duke of Alva, governor of the Spanish Netherlands, in 1567. He returned to Tournai, but then was forced to flee to Guernsey, Channel Islands, and on to Southampton. He is the ancestor of many British and American bearers of this surname. — This surname is listed (in the form de la Motte) in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America, referring to Jean Henri (de) La Motte from France (see Lamotte ).

variants.name: Moot
meanings: Dutch: metonymic occupational name for a fishmonger, from moot ‘fillet, slice, cut’.
Flemish: perhaps a variant of Motte . Alternatively, of the same origin as the name in 1 above. The surname Moot is very rare in Belgium.

variants.name: Moten
meanings: Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived by a fortified stronghold, Middle Dutch mote (see Motte 1).

variants.name: Mota
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Carlos, Manuel, Juan, Rafael, Fernando, Pedro, Armando, Guillermo, Jesus, Agustin.  Portuguese Joao, Duarte, Agostinho, Guilherme.
Spanish and Portuguese: topographic name for someone who livedby a fortified stronghold, from mot(t)a, a borrowing from OldFrench motte (see Motte 1).
Spanish: habitational name from any of the places in the provinces of Cuenca and Valladolid named Mota.

variants.name: Lamotte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Berthe, Gaston, Laurent, Nicolle, Philippe.

French and Walloon: topographic name for someone who lived by a motte (i.e. a protective mound surrounding a fortified stronghold), a variant of Motte 1 with the definite article la; or a habitational name from any of the places in France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Lamotte or La Motte. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium. Compare Lamothe and Delamotte .

History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (in the form La Motte, along with the altered form Lamott) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America (in the forms de la Motte and La Motte). The Huguenot ancestor was Jean Henri (de) La Motte from France, who arrived in SC in the middle of the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: -Wood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: mainly a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a wood, from Middle English wode ‘wood’ (Old English wudu). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, such as French Bois and Polish Les .
English: in a few cases, a nickname for an eccentric or perhaps a violent person, from Middle English wode ‘frenzied, wild’ (Old English wōd).
Americanized form of French   Gadbois  .

variants.name: Woode
meanings: English: variant of   Wood  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 312, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

variants.name: Bois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Woods
meanings: English: variant of Wood with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.

Irish: adopted as a translation of Ó Cuill ‘descendant of Coll’ (see Quill ), or in Ulster of Mac Con Coille ‘son of Cú Choille’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the wood’, which has also been mistranslated Cox , as if formed with coileach ‘cock, rooster’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Motte -Wood was born about 1564, in Lyme Regis, Dorset, England, her father, Richard Edward Atwood, was 25 and her mother, Ellen Bosworth, was 30. She married John Wood VI about 1588, in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died about 1636, in Leicester, Leicestershire, England, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Motte -Wood est née environ 1564, à Lyme Regis, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Richard Edward Atwood, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Ellen Bosworth, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé John Wood VI environ 1588, à Hertford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1636, à Leicester, Leicestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Motte -Wood um 1564 in Lyme Regis, Dorset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Edward Atwood 25 und ihre Mutter Ellen Bosworth 30. Sie hat um 1588 in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom John Wood VI geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1636 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Leicester, Leicestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Motte -Wood è nata il circa 1564 nel Lyme Regis, Dorset, England, sua padre, Richard Edward Atwood, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Ellen Bosworth, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato John Wood VI circa 1588, nel Hertford, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì circa 1636, nel Leicester, Leicestershire, England, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Motte -Woodが約1564年にイギリスドールセトリメ レジスで生まれた時，父Richard Edward Atwoodは25歳，母Ellen Bosworthは30歳であった。約1588年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアハートフォードでJohn Wood VIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1636年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアレアイセスターで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Motte -Wood(이)가 대략 1564년 영국 도르세트 림 레지스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Edward Atwood(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ellen Bosworth(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 대략 1588년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 허트퍼드에서 John Wood VI(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1636년 영국 레이스스터실르 레이스스터에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.
